items.title: Rogers — Public Affairs
items.meta: $30K CAD · created May 11, advanced to Proposal May 22
items.rationale: Rogers is an existing Tier 1 telco partner. Public Affairs is a new internal team — same logo, new buyer. Janessa on vacation until May 27.
items.coaching: Textbook expansion play. Follow up May 27–28 when Janessa is back.

items.title: Felix — Affordable Pricing Campaign
items.meta: $20K CAD · created May 21, advanced to Proposal May 22
items.rationale: Prior Felix campaign just wrapped (3-contact wrap-up thread this week). New budget cycle, fast-moving. Sara Ross booked a discovery call the same week.
items.coaching: Strong post-campaign expansion. Sara is the champion — keep her close.

items.title: Alan — CWHL Phase 2
items.meta: $15K CAD · created and reached Proposal May 22
items.rationale: Explicit Phase 2 of an existing CWHL partnership — Q&A placements, web articles, and social video. Jacob Batist intro call held this week.
items.coaching: EOW noted original estimate was ~$10K; deal logged at $15K — good upsell signal.

items.title: Lums AI
items.meta: $4K CAD · created and reached Proposal May 20
items.rationale: New logo converted from a warm intro (Marion Kesteloot, relationship_intro track). Only true net-new proposal this week.
items.coaching: Budget meeting June 2. Long sales cycle expected — flag if it stalls past June.

items.title: Where we are
items.rationale: 23 proposals logged since the plan started (~April 1). At 5/week pace, ~40 would be expected by now — so we're running behind on the cumulative count.

items.title: Required weekly rate
items.rationale: Need 5.3 proposals/week from here to hit 186 by year-end. This week's 4 is 1.3 below the required rate.
items.coaching: The gap is manageable but compounding. Two strong weeks (6+ proposals each) would put us back on track.

items.title: Sensitivity check
items.rationale: We'd land at ~116 by year-end — roughly $380K short of the $1M revenue target.
items.coaching: Cold outreach is the lever. Zero cold emails this week means no new logos entering the funnel.

pipeline_dollars.title: Pipeline $ generated this week
pipeline_dollars.summary: $69K CAD · avg $17.3K per proposal (matches H2 plan avg)

items.title: Rogers — Public Affairs
items.rationale: Largest single deal this week. Rogers is a proven buyer — high close-rate expectation.

items.title: Felix — Affordable Pricing Campaign
items.rationale: Strong follow-on deal size. Prior campaign likely in the $15–20K range.

items.title: Alan — CWHL Phase 2
items.rationale: Right at the H2 plan average deal size ($17.5K). Well-scoped.

items.title: Lums AI
items.rationale: Well below average deal size for a net-new logo.
items.coaching: Net-new deals are trending small. Tier 1 cold prospecting (banks, insurers, telcos) would bring larger first-deal sizes.

items.title: 7 inbound calendar bookings (30 min with Michelle)
items.meta: Abby Fong (TD), Flora Berwick (RBC), Sara Ross (Felix), Jacob Batist (Alan), Nikita Shivdasani (SP PR), Denise Chow, Catch-up
items.rationale: Self-booked via Michelle's calendar link. Strong inbound conversion — EL and L+L programmes are driving people to book.
items.coaching: Abby Fong (TD) and Flora Berwick (RBC) are particularly high-value — both tied to active deal threads.

items.title: 3 existing account meetings
items.meta: The Peak / RBC (Jun 2), The Peak <> TD (May 26), The Peak <> Bell (May 28)
items.rationale: RBC and TD are in active pipeline. Bell AI Fabric ($50K) is in Proposal stage — strong renewal/expansion candidate.

items.title: 5 relationship / intro meetings
items.meta: Laura McEnery (Fintel Connect), Jordan Lavin (Nutrilawn), Alena Atkinson (Burson), Olivia ×2
items.rationale: Warm top-of-funnel. Alena at Burson is especially valuable — Burson is the most-touched prospect in the file (8+ emails, $9K EL deal in Contract Sent).

items.title: 2 net-new discovery meetings
items.meta: Discovery Feels Human Again, The Peak <> FH
items.rationale: New contacts, no prior thread. Good signs of organic inbound.

items.title: 1 internal coordination
items.meta: FW: Earned Media Monthly + IM Strategy (May 19)
items.rationale: Internal planning session — not counted in business meeting total.

items.title: Zero cold emails this week
items.rationale: All 71 outbound emails this week were warm: EL nominations, L+L scheduling, active pipeline follow-ups, relationship intros. No first 1:1 outreach to a non-customer contact.
items.coaching: The Emerging Leaders and Lunch & Learn engine is excellent at warming up existing contacts, but it doesn't replace cold prospecting. Net-new logos require cold reach. Recommend blocking 30 min/day for cold outreach from the bank comms list.

items.title: Priority sectors with 0 touches
items.meta: MBA programs · Insurance comms · ETF providers · Betakit/The Logic advertisers
items.rationale: Four of the named H2 target sectors had zero outreach this week. These are sectors Michelle identified as strategic for net-new growth.
items.coaching: Pick 2 contacts per zero-sector next week and do first outreach. Even 8 cold emails/week across these sectors would open 2–3 new conversations per month.

items.title: Pre-sale program prospecting
items.rationale: Emerging Leaders nominations (18 emails to Burson, Dialogue, #paid, Google, Council of Canadian Innovators, NarrativeXPR, SP PR) + Lunch & Learn scheduling (8 emails to Bensimon Byrne, Category Comms, Zeno, Agnostic) + Peak Perspectives Op-Ed (2 emails to Habiba/RBC).
items.coaching: Most efficient prospecting motion in the week. EL → L+L → deal is the funnel that's working. Burson is the clearest example.

items.title: Active pipeline touches
items.rationale: Smart Money / TD Auto (5 emails to Kiana Crummy) + TD Business Banking (4 emails to Abby Fong) = 9 emails on a single deal thread. Also Tangerine via Rethink, Capital One via OMC, CWHL/Alan, Fitzrovia/RBC agency, B&R RFP.
items.coaching: TD Smart Money is the hottest thread — 9 emails in one week across two contacts suggests a proposal is imminent (next 1–2 weeks).

items.title: Relationship intros
items.rationale: Habiba/RBC warm intro (4 emails, progressed to Op-Ed pitch), Jordan Lavin, Laura McEnery (Fintel/RBC WestJet deal), Annette MacIntyre, Google visit follow-up (Iran Karimian).

items.title: Mid-sale coordination
items.rationale: Felix wrap-up (3 contacts: Sara Ross, Candy Lee, Mike), RBC performance review (Flora Berwick), Scotia HISA sponsored content (Elizabeth Glassen), Weber Shandwick campaign (GT).
items.coaching: 11% of nurture volume — healthy. Felix wrap should close out within 1–2 weeks, freeing up capacity.

items.title: Nurture leads (warm, no deal)
items.rationale: Zeno (Dan LeBaron, media kit sent post-meeting), Weber Shandwick (Nancy D'Souza, post-L+L gift card), Deloitte (Ryan, rescheduling), NATIONAL PR (Emma Clarkin, media kit + L+L pitch).
items.coaching: Zeno and NATIONAL PR are conversion candidates. Track to proposal within 4 weeks.

items.title: Major account expansion
items.rationale: TD new team (Ada Rampersaud, drinks invite via Brooke intro), Bell (Sasha Omerasevic, coffee rescheduled), Rogers (Jenessa Crognali, post-call follow-up while on vacation).
items.coaching: Ada at TD is the most interesting — she's a new team introduction that could open a parallel deal track. Follow up after the TD meeting this week.
